w:r.w:t: AI-Powered Rooftop Solar Feasibility & ROI Platform

SolarScan AI is a browser-based SaaS tool that lets homeowners, commercial property owners, and solar installers instantly evaluate rooftop solar potential for any address in India — without requiring a physical site visit or specialised engineering knowledge.

A user enters an address or pincode, traces their rooftop boundary on satellite imagery, configures panel preferences and electricity tariffs, and receives a full financial feasibility report including projected ROI, payback period, monthly energy harvest, government subsidy eligibility, and carbon savings — all within minutes.
The Indian rooftop solar market is growing at 30%+ YoY, yet the sales funnel is broken at the top:
Homeowners have no reliable way to self-assess if solar is viable for their specific roof before inviting a vendor.
Solar installers spend 2–4 hours per lead on manual site surveys, burning cost on unqualified prospects.
Misinformation on pricing and subsidies (especially PM Surya Ghar) creates distrust and stalls decisions.
The pre-purchase research phase is dominated by generic content — nothing personalised to an individual rooftop.
The gap is not awareness — it is conviction. Homeowners want quantified proof before committing. SolarScan AI turns a satellite map into a personalised business case.
SolarScan AI bridges the research-to-intent gap by giving any user a satellite-based, AI-assisted rooftop analysis in under 5 minutes. The platform combines:
Satellite imagery + manual rooftop tracing for precise roof area calculation.
Configurable panel layout engine with real-time panel count, shading, and setback modelling.
Financial modelling engine (tariff-aware ROI, EMI, subsidy) calibrated for India.
A paywall-gated PDF report that converts interest into a paid transaction.
Urban/semi-urban homeowner, 30–55 yrs, owns flat or independent house, moderate solar awareness
Understand if solar saves money on my specific home
Too many vendor calls, no credible self-serve tool
Field sales at a solar company, runs 10–30 leads/week, needs to qualify fast
Pre-qualify leads before site visit, share professional report
Manual calculations, no leave-behind document for client
No scalable tool; relies on consultant quotes
Energy consultants and auditors creating client proposals.
Financial institutions running solar loan viability checks.
Students and researchers requiring rooftop dataset proxies.
Features are classified Must-Have (required for MVP), Should-Have (target for v1.1), and Nice-to-Have (future roadmap).
Animated capability highlights (predictive yields, layouts, setbacks)
Sets user expectations and builds trust
Reduces drop-off by showing what comes next
Social proof; can launch with placeholder copy
Delight layer; remove if perf is an issue on mobile
3D Earth Globe intro (Three.js) with scroll-zoom transition to 2D map
Core utility; fallback source is critical
Rooftop tracing tool (click-to-draw polygon, Turf.js area calc)
Precise area is the foundation of all downstream maths
Sandbox Metro Jumps (Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai)
Demo & onboarding; lets users explore without searching
Affects output and cost calculations directly
Row alignment options (roof-perimeter vs geographical South)
Changes panel count and layout density
Outer boundary setback & maintenance walkway gap inputs
Required for real-world installable area accuracy
Live cell-level grid overlay on map
Large TAM on mobile; complex to implement well
ROI Tuning Console (tariff, active panel count, panel grade)
Dynamic recompute is core product value
Clearest single output metric for homeowners
Gross cost, PM Surya Ghar subsidy estimate (₹78K cap), net cost, EMI
Carbon offset card (tonnes CO₂ + tree equivalent)
Revenue; must integrate real payment before full launch
Sufficient for MVP demo; replace with real gateway pre-launch
Shareable results link (unique URL per scan)
Installer lead-gen form on results page
System size recommendation engine (AI-suggested optimal config)
Live electricity tariff lookup by DISCOM
Remove manual entry friction; API required
Enters address in hero search bar or clicks a Sandbox Metro
Geocodes address → navigates to /map, centres satellite view
Globe fades out, 2D satellite map fades in
Clicks corners of rooftop to draw polygon
Live polygon renders; Turf.js computes area in m²
Sets tilt angle, outer setback, walkway gap
Usable area updates live; reflected in panel grid
Chooses 550W premium, geographical South alignment, portrait
Cell grid re-renders on map with final panel count
System records tariff, enables 'View Report' CTA
Free preview shows summary metrics; detailed sections blurred with paywall overlay
Checkout modal opens (UPI QR or card form)
Paywall lifts; all sections visible; PDF export button activates
jsPDF generates formatted PDF; browser download triggers
User lands on / but doesn't search → retargeting pixel fires; landing page handles objections inline.
User traces roof but abandons before Step 3 → local state (or session storage) preserves polygon; re-entry resumes from last step.
User hits paywall and drops → email capture before paywall is a Should-Have to enable re-engagement.
Installer shares a results link → recipient sees free preview; CTA drives their own scan → new paid conversion.
The MVP is the smallest working product that lets a paying user go from address → traced roof → financial report → purchased PDF download in one unbroken session.
Functional landing page with address search and sandbox metros.
Globe → Satellite map transition with functional rooftop tracing (polygon + area calc).
3-step layout configurator producing a valid panel count and kWp system size.
Results page with all financial metrics computed correctly (tariff-aware, subsidy-capped).
Recharts: 25-year cashflow chart + monthly harvest bar chart.
Paywall overlay with simulated payment UI sufficient for demo; Razorpay integration required before real user payments.
PDF export (jsPDF) covering all core report sections.
Mobile-readable layout for results page (full mobile editor is v1.1).
User accounts / authentication — no login; session-based only in MVP.
Saved scans history — no database persistence for user data.
Real payment gateway — simulated checkout suffices for investor demo; required before public launch.
Multi-roof tracing or L-shaped polygon merge.
6. What We Are Deliberately NOT Building in V1
This section exists to prevent scope creep. These are intentional exclusions, not oversights.
Web-first is faster to ship; covers majority of urban Indian users via Chrome mobile
Cost-prohibitive; Google/Esri tiles are sufficient for rooftop tracing accuracy
Requires physical inspection; outside our software scope and creates liability
High regulatory variance by DISCOM; requires legal review and ops overhead
B2B complexity; shifts us from tool to marketplace — different business model
Language localisation requires dedicated QA pass; English covers urban TAM for MVP
Adds backend complexity and DPDPA compliance surface area without clear V1 revenue impact
Requires API integration with PM portal; not publicly available; high compliance risk
Out of core solar ROI use case; distraction for MVP
Average session duration on map page
Map page load time < 3 seconds on 4G (Lighthouse LCP).
Globe → map transition completes without crash on mid-range Android (Moto G-class).
Zero instances of incorrect subsidy calculation (₹78K cap logic must be unit tested).
Results page financial model margin of error vs. manual calc ≤ 2%.
Number of paid scans completed per week. This single metric captures activation quality, product utility, and revenue simultaneously.
Google Satellite via Leaflet; Esri as fallback. API key costs must be budgeted by monthly tile request volume.
Currently inferred from tilt/location heuristics. NASA POWER API integration is deferred but referenced in marketing copy — align engineering on actual data source before launch.
All calculations are estimates. Legal disclaimer required on results page: 'Actual yields may vary ±15–20% based on local shading, panel degradation, and DISCOM charges.'
PM Surya Ghar cap of ₹78,000 based on 2024 scheme rules. Needs quarterly review as scheme parameters change.
Simulated checkout in MVP. Razorpay or Cashfree integration (UPI + cards) required before charging real users. GST compliance needed from Day 1.
Chrome 90+, Safari 15+, Firefox 90+. IE11 not supported. Three.js globe degrades gracefully on unsupported WebGL devices.
No user PII stored in MVP (session-only). When accounts launch, DPDPA 2023 compliance framework required.
Is the NASA POWER API actually integrated for irradiance, or are we using a static lookup table? Marketing copy must match reality.
Which payment gateway — Razorpay or Cashfree? Decision affects UPI QR flow and refund policy.
Do we add email capture before the paywall to enable re-engagement flows?
What is the legal liability position on financial projections? Do we need a CA to sign off on the model?
C&I pricing tier: what does 'Commercial & Industrial' actually unlock vs. Residential? Needs feature differentiation.
Mobile editor: 'Should-Have' for v1.1, but do we block the map page on mobile with a soft nudge to desktop?
Subsidy scheme parameters change periodically — who owns the quarterly review and update process?
Marketing copy (NASA data, 'predictive yields') overpromises actual model accuracy
Audit copy vs. actual tech stack before launch; add accuracy disclaimers
Rooftop tracing is too friction-heavy for non-technical homeowners
User test with 5 non-technical users before launch; consider guided walkthrough overlay
Satellite imagery resolution insufficient for small urban plots
Test in tier-2 cities; offer manual area input as fallback
Google Maps tile API costs spike with scale
Implement tile request caching; set API billing alerts; Esri fallback
Build Razorpay in parallel with MVP; don't wait for perfect UI
PM Surya Ghar scheme rules change mid-quarter
Modularise subsidy calculation; flag in UI as 'based on current scheme rules'
Three.js globe causes OOM crash on low-end devices
Graceful degradation: skip globe, go straight to 2D map if WebGL unavailable
Kilowatt-peak — the rated output of a solar installation under standard test conditions
Distribution Company — the regional electricity utility that manages grid connection and tariffs
Indian government rooftop solar subsidy scheme offering up to ₹78,000 for residential installations
Open-source geospatial analysis library used to compute polygon areas from map coordinates
Mandatory clear space between the roof edge and the nearest solar panel row, required for safety and maintenance
Commercial & Industrial — the non-residential solar segment, typically ≥ 10 kWp systems
Interactive panel on the results page that lets users adjust tariff, panel count, and grade to recalculate financials in real time
One-time payment (₹149) to unlock a single full scan report, as opposed to the monthly Pro subscription
